"The transformation of agents being applied to every single UX problem is the biggest opportunity in the world right now."

Kain goes deep on agent harnesses, context windows, removing humans from the loop, and what Infinex looks like with an AI co-pilot.

Full conversation: https://t.co/3K3U0I7Npp

tweets.text:
"The transformation of agents being applied to every single UX problem is the biggest opportunity in the world right now."

Kain goes deep on agent harnesses, context windows, removing humans from the loop, and what Infinex looks like with an AI co-pilot.

Full conversation:

Infinex spent three years solving crypto UX. Then agents changed the question entirely.

Now it's not about better buttons, it's about having an agent that uses 20+ DEX integrations, every API, and your entire transaction history to work for you.

Every form factor of interaction with technology just got nuked by agents.

Kain: "No one is going to do all of their own crypto stuff anymore."

Go back to your wallet in 2021. Pick a random transaction. Tell me why you did it. You can't, but an agent never forgets.

note_tweet.text:
The AI startup market looks crowded. Kain says that's the signal, not the noise.

"Vercel can't decide if they're an agent API gateway or a hosting platform. The largest companies down to startups have no clue how to handle this."

Early DeFi was the same. Chaos = infinite opportunity for anyone willing to show up and build.

tweets.text:
The AI startup market looks crowded. Kain says that's the signal, not the noise.

"Vercel can't decide if they're an agent API gateway or a hosting platform. The largest companies down to startups have no clue how to handle this."

Early DeFi was the same. Chaos = infinite

Kain's hot take: eight more months of sideways, capitulation by year-end, four-year cycle validated again.

2018 built DeFi. 2022 built the infrastructure layer. 2026 builds the agent layer.

"The noise floor drops. You can really focus on what's actually needed."

“Even a bad idea in AI is going to be more successful than a good idea in crypto right now”

note_tweet.text:

The difference: guard rails. Your agent proposes. You approve. Daily limits. Constraints. You're not just handing over a private key

tweets.text:
So what happens when you give those agents access to your crypto?

Infinex's MCP server gives any agent the full crypto stack: swap, bridge, perps, DeFi, prediction markets. One install.

The difference: guard rails. Your agent proposes. You approve. Daily limits. Constraints.

An agent starts at 100 IQ. Within 3 minutes it's at 80. Within half an hour it's at 20.

Kain's been building his own coding harness to solve this. Keep the context window small and the agent stays sharp. Spawn, execute, wipe, repeat

note_tweet.text:
The only tokens that will outperform next cycle are the ones that figure out how to make people want to hold spot again.

Staking, tokenomics redesign, real yield. Whatever the mechanism, the projects that solve spot holding will go from 90% down to 500x up.

Everything else stays a 48-hour trade

tweets.text:
The only tokens that will outperform next cycle are the ones that figure out how to make people want to hold spot again.

Staking, tokenomics redesign, real yield. Whatever the mechanism, the projects that solve spot holding will go from 90% down to 500x up.

Everything else

The altcoin market is structurally broken

Perps are the baseline now. Not spot. The UNI fee switch ran from $5 to $10 on pure perp activity, then round-tripped to $3. Zero spot buying.

Kain's take: There will not be a broad market-agnostic rally in alts ever again

Recently @kaiynne sat down with @VirtualBacon to talk about why the altcoin market is structurally broken, how agentic loops are changing everything, and why Infinex is building for a world where your agent manages your crypto.

Full interview and 🧵↓ https://t.co/AdUoSGV1Sg

urls.title: Infinex
urls.description: Meet a new kind of wallet

tweets.text:
When you use Private Send, Houdini routes funds through a network of compliant exchange partners with a dual exchange hop and a random L1 in between.

There's no visible link between the sending and receiving wallet.

Try it and let us know your feedback ↓https://t.co/VcdtBT56i9

TL;DL

Why @HoudiniSwap?

→ $2.5B processed across ~1M transactions
→ 3+ years live through full market cycles
→ Natively multi-chain
→ Zero funds ever lost
→ Fully compliant

They've run three separate $50K bounties. Nobody could trace a transaction.

We hosted a Spaces with @HoudiniSwap this morning to explore crypto privacy, how Houdini works and why we integrated it at Infinex 🎙️

https://t.co/aXBHlUhQqg

How to use Private Send on Infinex:

1. Open Labs → Private Send
2. Enter the recipient address
3. Pick your token and amount
4. Choose if you want to send or swidge
5. Review and send

That's it. You can swap and send from one flow.

Blockchains are public by default.

Every transfer creates a permanent, traceable record linking your wallet to every address you've interacted with.

Private Send breaks that link. The recipient wallet gets the funds. No on-chain trail shows where they came from.

Private Send beta is live for all users.

Send crypto. Without exposing your financial history.

Incognito Mode, powered by @HoudiniSwap 🔮

How it works ↓ https://t.co/IWZutn0qup

All vouchers already earned - including 1M FDV vouchers - remain valid and redeemable under existing rules.

No more $2,500 cap and no more random allocation

We are talking to the…

note_tweet.text:
We got the sale wrong.

We tried to balance existing Patron holders, new participants, and fair distribution all at once and the result was a sale that (almost) nobody wanted to participate in.

Retail hates the lock.
Whales hate the cap.
Everyone hates the complexity.

To our community: you've been telling us. And you were right. And we apologize for how we handled this.

So, here's what's changing:

1/ Caps removed. No more $2,500 maximum. If you want to put in more, you can. We're done trying to guess the right number. The market will decide.

2/ Bottom-up fill. We're switching from random allocation to a "max-min fair allocation", also known as water filling. Everyone's allocation rises equally until it's full or supply runs out. Any excess contribution is refunded.

3/ Patron preference stays. Patrons still get priority on allocations, but we're waiting until the end of the sale to finalize exactly how - once we have real data on total demand instead of guessing.

We're keeping the lock. We still believe lockups create the long-term alignment for those who believe in the product.

Which brings us to the product – we haven’t spent enough time showing and telling you why you should use Infinex. Which changes now.

So here's what we've actually built - a new kind of crypto app that feels like a CEX but is self-custodial:

→ A best-in-class swap and bridge aggregator - live on 24 chains, including OG networks like ZEC, XRP and DOGE that are hard to access elsewhere

→ A perps trading experience built from the ground up by @0xEquinox_ and team, starting as a frontend for @HyperliquidX (#2 builder code), with @Lighter_xyz and @synthetix_io coming soon

→ A browser extension (led by the Herculean efforts of @ben_kurrek) that lets you take your Infinex account to any onchain app - currently 5★ on Chrome Web Store

→ Passkey-secured accounts powered by @turnkeyhq - no seed phrases, no custody responsibility pushed onto users

→ Unified portfolio and wallet management, giving you access to all your crypto wallets across devices

→ An unmatched customer service team headed by @Khaleesi_98

We created Infinex because crypto should be easier to use, and we made a big dent in our goal last year.

We have a massive feature roadmap planned for 2026, including a native mobile app, private sends, hardware wallet support, and much more.

We've built an amazing team and a compelling product, and we’re here to build for the long term.

We've spent a lot of time trying to get your attention – for better and worse.

Now we're asking you to try the product and make up your own mind.
